def: horizontal structural member that carries loads primarily in bending
ex: The steel {{beam}} spans between two supporting columns.

def: vertical structural member that carries axial compressive loads downward
ex: Each {{column}} transfers the floor load to the foundation.

def: framework of members arranged in triangles to span large distances
ex: The roof {{truss}} carries the weight of the tiles.

def: wall that supports structural loads from floors or roof above
ex: Do not remove that {{load-bearing wall}} without proper support.

def: concrete strengthened with embedded steel bars to resist tension
ex: The bridge deck is made of {{reinforced concrete}}.

def: structural skeleton of steel beams and columns supporting a building
ex: The high-rise uses a bolted {{steel frame}}.

def: lowest structure transferring building loads safely into the ground
ex: The {{foundation}} distributes loads to the bearing soil.

def: enlarged base of a foundation spreading load onto soil
ex: A wide {{footing}} reduces the soil bearing pressure.

def: beam fixed at one end and projecting freely at the other
ex: The balcony is built as a reinforced {{cantilever}}.

def: internal moment causing a member to bend under load
ex: The maximum {{bending moment}} occurs at midspan.

def: internal force acting parallel to a member's cross-section
ex: The {{shear force}} is greatest near the supports.

def: force acting along the longitudinal axis of a member
ex: The column carries a large {{axial load}} in compression.

def: permanent static load from the structure's own weight
ex: The {{dead load}} includes beams, slabs and finishes.

def: variable load from occupants, furniture and movable objects
ex: Office floors are designed for a specified {{live load}}.

def: clear distance between two consecutive supports of a member
ex: The bridge has a main {{span}} of eighty metres.

def: vertical displacement of a member under applied loading
ex: Excessive {{deflection}} can crack the ceiling finishes.

def: process of determining forces and deformations in a structure
ex: The engineer performed a full {{structural analysis}} of the frame.

def: route by which loads travel through a structure to ground
ex: A clear {{load path}} ensures stability under gravity loads.

def: flat horizontal concrete element forming floors or roofs
ex: The floor {{slab}} is two hundred millimetres thick.

def: large main beam supporting smaller beams and heavy loads
ex: The main {{girder}} carries all the floor beams.

def: one of parallel beams supporting a floor or ceiling
ex: Timber {{joist}} spacing is usually four hundred millimetres.

def: beam spanning across the top of an opening
ex: A steel {{lintel}} supports the wall above the window.

def: sudden sideways failure of a slender member under compression
ex: Slender columns may fail by {{buckling}} before crushing.

def: geometric property measuring a section's resistance to bending
ex: A deeper beam has a larger {{moment of inertia}}.

def: maximum load a structure can carry before failure
ex: The test measured the beam's {{ultimate load}} capacity.

def: internal force that shortens or squeezes a member
ex: Concrete performs well under {{compression}} but poorly in tension.

def: internal force that stretches or pulls a member apart
ex: Steel reinforcement resists the {{tension}} in the beam.

def: internal force per unit area within a material
ex: The {{stress}} must stay below the material's yield limit.

def: relative change in length caused by applied stress
ex: The gauge measured the {{strain}} under increasing load.

def: stress at which a material begins permanent deformation
ex: Structural steel has a defined {{yield strength}}.

def: maximum load the soil can safely support
ex: The soil's {{bearing capacity}} governs the footing size.

def: long column driven into ground to transfer deep loads
ex: Each {{pile}} reaches the firm rock layer below.

def: wall built to hold back soil or water
ex: The {{retaining wall}} resists the lateral earth pressure.

def: temporary mould holding wet concrete until it hardens
ex: The {{formwork}} is removed after the concrete cures.

def: steel bar embedded in concrete to carry tension
ex: Workers tied the {{rebar}} before pouring the slab.

def: concrete with internal tensioned tendons improving load capacity
ex: The long-span bridge uses {{prestressed concrete}} girders.

def: maximum allowable deflection specified by design codes
ex: The beam satisfies the code {{deflection limit}}.

def: force developed at a support resisting applied loads
ex: The {{support reaction}} equals half the total load.

def: rigid joint transferring bending moment between members
ex: A welded {{moment connection}} joins the beam to the column.

def: joint allowing rotation but transferring no bending moment
ex: The brace uses a simple {{pinned connection}}.

def: diagonal members providing lateral stability to a frame
ex: Cross {{bracing}} resists the horizontal wind loads.

def: lateral pressure exerted by wind on a structure
ex: Tall buildings must resist significant {{wind load}}.

def: forces induced in a structure by earthquake ground motion
ex: Buildings in the region are designed for {{seismic load}}.

def: ratio of a structure's strength to expected load
ex: A high {{factor of safety}} protects against uncertainty.

def: ability of a structure to withstand loads without failing
ex: The fire threatened the building's {{structural integrity}}.

def: stiff wall resisting horizontal forces in a building
ex: Concrete {{shear wall}} stiffens the tower against wind.

def: a member's capacity to resist bending before failure
ex: The beam's {{flexural strength}} exceeds the design moment.

def: individual component carrying load within a structural system
ex: Every {{structural member}} was checked for capacity.

def: defined mix of loads applied simultaneously in design
ex: The worst {{load combination}} governs the beam size.

def: condition beyond which a structure fails to perform
ex: Design checks both ultimate and serviceability {{limit state}}.

def: concrete layer protecting reinforcement from corrosion and fire
ex: Adequate {{concrete cover}} prevents rebar from rusting.

def: localised shear failure where a column punches through a slab
ex: The flat slab was checked for {{punching shear}}.

def: gap allowing a structure to expand without cracking
ex: An {{expansion joint}} accommodates thermal movement in the bridge.

def: self-weight of a structure acting as permanent load
ex: The {{dead weight}} of the slab was included first.

def: connected system of beams and columns supporting a building
ex: The {{structural frame}} carries all gravity and lateral loads.

def: horizontal surface of a bridge carrying traffic loads
ex: The bridge {{deck}} rests on the main girders.

def: end support of a bridge resisting deck and earth loads
ex: Each {{abutment}} anchors the bridge to the embankment.

def: resistance of a member to deformation under load
ex: Greater {{stiffness}} reduces the beam's deflection.

def: ability of a material to deform significantly before fracture
ex: Steel's {{ductility}} gives warning before collapse.

def: twisting of a member caused by an applied torque
ex: The eccentric load induces {{torsion}} in the beam.

def: ratio of effective length to the section's radius of gyration
ex: A high {{slenderness ratio}} increases the risk of buckling.

def: rigid frame of columns and rafters spanning a single bay
ex: The warehouse uses a steel {{portal frame}}.

def: floor or roof plate distributing lateral loads to walls
ex: The floor {{diaphragm}} transfers wind load to the cores.

def: bolt embedded in concrete to secure a base plate
ex: The column base plate is fixed with {{anchor bolt}} groups.

def: steel plate spreading column load onto the foundation
ex: The {{base plate}} distributes the column force to concrete.

def: shape seen when a member is cut perpendicular to its axis
ex: The beam's {{cross-section}} determines its bending capacity.

def: line in a section where bending stress equals zero
ex: Bending stress is zero at the {{neutral axis}}.

def: load concentrated at a single location on a member
ex: A heavy machine applies a {{point load}} on the slab.

def: load spread continuously along a member's length
ex: The self-weight acts as a uniform {{distributed load}}.

def: downward movement of a foundation as soil compresses
ex: Differential {{settlement}} can crack the superstructure.

def: portion of a structure above the foundation level
ex: The {{superstructure}} rests on deep pile foundations.

def: multiplier applied to loads to ensure design safety
ex: A {{load factor}} increases the dead load in design.

def: member subjected to both axial load and bending
ex: The corner {{beam-column}} carries combined axial and moment forces.

def: Study of soil behaviour under stress, water, and load conditions
ex: Foundation design relies on {{soil mechanics}} principles.

def: Removal of soil or rock to form a hole or trench
ex: Deep {{excavation}} required shoring to prevent collapse.

def: Granular material like sand or gravel used in concrete
ex: Coarse {{aggregate}} improves the strength of concrete.

def: Binding powder that hardens to hold aggregates together
ex: Portland {{cement}} is mixed with water and sand.

def: Proportioned blend of cement, aggregate, and water for concrete
ex: The {{concrete mix}} was designed for high strength.

def: Bituminous material used to surface roads and pavements
ex: Fresh {{asphalt}} was laid across the highway.

def: Densifying soil by mechanical means to increase stability
ex: Proper {{compaction}} reduces settlement of the embankment.

def: Layer of soil lying beneath the surface topsoil
ex: The {{subsoil}} contained dense clay deposits.

def: Solid rock underlying loose soil and surface deposits
ex: Piles were driven down to the {{bedrock}}.

def: Deep foundation transferring loads to firm strata below
ex: A {{pile foundation}} supported the tower on soft ground.

def: Resistance of an inclined soil mass to failure or sliding
ex: Engineers assessed {{slope stability}} before excavation.

def: Water held below the surface in soil and rock pores
ex: High {{groundwater}} complicated the foundation works.

def: Ability of soil or rock to allow water to pass through
ex: Sandy soil has high {{permeability}} to water.

def: Measuring positions, distances, and levels of land features
ex: A topographic {{survey}} mapped the entire site.

def: Measuring height differences between points on the ground
ex: Precise {{levelling}} established the road gradient.

def: Instrument for measuring horizontal and vertical angles precisely
ex: The surveyor set up the {{theodolite}} over the peg.

def: Fixed reference point of known elevation for levelling
ex: Heights were measured from the ordnance {{benchmark}}.

def: Map line joining points of equal ground elevation
ex: Closely spaced {{contour line}}s indicate steep terrain.

def: Rate of slope or incline of a surface
ex: The road {{gradient}} was too steep for trucks.

def: Narrow shaft bored to investigate ground conditions
ex: Soil samples were taken from each {{borehole}}.

def: Permeable fabric used to reinforce or separate soil layers
ex: A {{geotextile}} was placed beneath the road base.

def: Maintaining moisture in concrete to develop full strength
ex: Proper {{curing}} prevented cracking in the slab.

def: Steel bars embedded in concrete to resist tension
ex: Steel {{reinforcement}} strengthened the concrete beam.

def: Material used to refill an excavation around a structure
ex: Compacted {{backfill}} surrounded the buried pipe.

def: Raised bank of earth built to carry a road or rail
ex: The railway {{embankment}} was built from compacted clay.

def: Soil's resistance to sliding along an internal surface
ex: Clay's {{shear strength}} decreases when saturated.

def: Gradual soil compression as pore water is expelled
ex: Clay {{consolidation}} caused slow long-term settlement.

def: Loss of soil strength when saturated sand behaves as liquid
ex: The earthquake triggered soil {{liquefaction}} beneath foundations.

def: Attractive force binding fine soil particles together
ex: Clay owes its {{cohesion}} to particle attraction.

def: Range of moisture over which soil remains plastic
ex: A high {{plasticity index}} indicates expansive clay.

def: Distribution of particle sizes within a soil sample
ex: Sieve analysis revealed the soil's {{grading}} curve.

def: Test separating soil particles by passing through sieves
ex: A {{sieve analysis}} determined the sand grading.

def: Upper level below which the ground is saturated
ex: The high {{water table}} required continuous dewatering.

def: Lowering groundwater to allow dry excavation work
ex: Well-point {{dewatering}} kept the trench dry.

def: Field test measuring fresh concrete's workability and consistency
ex: The {{slump test}} confirmed the mix was workable.

def: Ease with which fresh concrete can be placed and compacted
ex: Adding water improved the concrete's {{workability}}.

def: Chemical added to concrete to modify its properties
ex: An {{admixture}} accelerated the concrete's setting time.

def: Maximum compressive load a material withstands before failure
ex: The cube's {{compressive strength}} exceeded design requirements.

def: Maximum tension a material resists before it breaks
ex: Concrete has low {{tensile strength}} without reinforcement.

def: Native soil layer prepared to support a pavement
ex: The {{subgrade}} was compacted before laying the base.

def: Layer of aggregate beneath the road surface course
ex: Crushed stone formed the pavement {{base course}}.

def: Small loose stones used as construction aggregate
ex: A layer of {{gravel}} improved site drainage.

def: Fine soil particles between sand and clay in size
ex: River deposits left a thick layer of {{silt}}.

def: Fine-grained cohesive soil that becomes plastic when wet
ex: Expansive {{clay}} swells when it absorbs water.

def: Proportion of void space within a soil or rock
ex: High {{porosity}} allows the soil to store water.

def: Testing soil properties directly in the ground
ex: {{In-situ testing}} avoided disturbing the soil sample.

def: Pushing a cone into soil to measure resistance
ex: The {{cone penetration test}} profiled the soft layers.

def: Electronic instrument measuring angles and distances for surveying
ex: The {{total station}} recorded coordinates automatically.

def: Surveying method fixing points using measured triangles
ex: {{Triangulation}} established control points across the valley.

def: Reference surface from which elevations are measured
ex: All heights were referenced to a common {{datum}}.

def: Measured distance along a route's centre line
ex: The culvert was located at {{chainage}} 2+400.

def: Balancing excavated soil against material needed for embankments
ex: The {{cut and fill}} calculation minimised earth haulage.

def: Compacting soil or concrete by repeated firm blows
ex: {{Tamping}} the base removed trapped air voids.

def: Compacted stone layer supporting a road pavement
ex: The {{aggregate base}} distributed traffic loads evenly.

def: Black viscous binder used in asphalt road surfacing
ex: Hot {{bitumen}} bound the aggregate in the mix.

def: Ratio of void volume to solid volume in soil
ex: A low {{void ratio}} means dense, stable soil.

def: Weight of water expressed relative to dry soil
ex: Optimum {{moisture content}} gives maximum compaction density.

def: Moisture boundaries defining soil's plastic and liquid states
ex: The {{Atterberg limits}} classified the clay's plasticity.

def: Erosion of soil around foundations by flowing water
ex: Bridge piers were protected against river {{scour}}.

def: Loose stone placed to protect surfaces from erosion
ex: {{Riprap}} armoured the embankment against wave action.

def: Grid-like polymer used to reinforce soil and pavements
ex: A {{geogrid}} reinforced the weak subgrade soil.

def: Uppermost fertile soil layer stripped before construction
ex: The {{topsoil}} was stockpiled for later landscaping.

def: Horizontal force soil exerts on a retaining structure
ex: The wall was designed for active {{lateral earth pressure}}.

def: Laboratory test finding soil's optimum compaction moisture and density
ex: The {{proctor test}} defined the target field density.

def: Injecting fluid material into soil or rock to strengthen it
ex: {{Grouting}} sealed the fractures in the rock mass.

def: Temporary support holding excavation walls from collapsing
ex: Steel {{shoring}} braced the deep trench walls.

def: Range and proportion of particle sizes in aggregate
ex: Well-graded {{gradation}} produces denser, stronger concrete.

def: Measuring concrete ingredients by weight or volume
ex: Accurate {{batching}} ensured consistent concrete quality.

def: Slow flow of water through soil or a barrier
ex: {{Seepage}} through the dam was carefully monitored.

def: Gathering terrain data from aircraft or satellite sensors
ex: {{Remote sensing}} mapped the terrain before ground surveys.

def: Temporary framework of poles and boards giving workers safe access.
ex: Workers erected {{scaffolding}} around the facade for the repairs.

def: Steel rod embedded in concrete to resist tensile stresses.
ex: They tied each {{reinforcement bar}} before pouring the slab.

def: System of channels and pipes removing surplus surface or ground water.
ex: Poor {{drainage}} caused water to pool along the roadway.

def: Network of pipes and works that carries away sewage and wastewater.
ex: The district upgraded its ageing {{sewerage}} to prevent overflows.

def: Long line of connected pipes conveying liquids or gases over distance.
ex: Engineers inspected the {{pipeline}} for corrosion and leaks.

def: Covered channel or pipe carrying water beneath a road or railway.
ex: A blocked {{culvert}} flooded the field after heavy rain.

def: Hard surfaced layer forming a road, path or parking area.
ex: The worn {{pavement}} was milled and resurfaced with asphalt.

def: Raised edge of stone or concrete bordering a road or pavement.
ex: The team set a new {{kerb}} along the widened footway.

def: Structure spanning an obstacle to carry traffic, people or services.
ex: The cable-stayed {{bridge}} spans the river in a single reach.

def: Barrier built across a watercourse to hold back and store water.
ex: The concrete {{dam}} regulates flow to the downstream turbines.

def: Underground passage bored through rock or soil for transport or services.
ex: A boring machine drove the {{tunnel}} beneath the mountain ridge.

def: Horizontal and vertical route a road follows across the terrain.
ex: Surveyors revised the {{road alignment}} to ease the sharp bend.

def: Measured inspection of a site recording levels, features and boundaries.
ex: A detailed {{site survey}} preceded the foundation design work.

def: Detailed technical drawing showing how a structure is to be built.
ex: The foreman checked the {{blueprint}} before positioning the columns.

def: Professional who estimates and controls construction costs and quantities.
ex: The {{quantity surveyor}} priced the works from the drawings.

def: Document listing materials and works with quantities for tender pricing.
ex: Contractors submitted rates against the {{bill of quantities}}.

def: Drawing detailing load-bearing elements such as beams and columns.
ex: The {{structural drawing}} specified the beam sizes and reinforcement.

def: Manufacture of building components off-site for rapid on-site assembly.
ex: {{Prefabrication}} of the panels cut the construction time sharply.

def: Treatment or membrane preventing water from penetrating a structure.
ex: The basement {{waterproofing}} kept the walls dry all winter.

def: Access shaft letting workers reach underground sewers or utility lines.
ex: Crews lifted the {{manhole}} cover to inspect the sewer below.

def: Sliding gate controlling the flow of water in a channel.
ex: Operators raised the {{sluice gate}} to release the reservoir water.

def: Structure releasing excess water safely from a dam or reservoir.
ex: Flood water poured down the {{spillway}} away from the dam.

def: Large artificial lake storing water for supply or power.
ex: The {{reservoir}} supplies drinking water to the whole region.

def: Bridge-like channel carrying water across a valley or low ground.
ex: The old {{aqueduct}} still carries the canal over the gorge.

def: Intermediate vertical support carrying a bridge span between abutments.
ex: Each {{pier}} of the viaduct stands in the shallow river.

def: View showing a structure cut through at right angles.
ex: The {{cross section}} shows the pavement layers and their depths.

def: Force or weight a structure must carry and resist.
ex: The beam was designed for the full traffic {{load}}.

def: Test measuring the workability and consistency of fresh concrete.
ex: The {{slump}} test showed the mix was too wet.

def: Levelling wet concrete or mortar to a flat, even surface.
ex: After pouring, the crew began {{screeding}} the floor level.

def: Construction using units of brick or stone bonded with mortar.
ex: The old {{masonry}} arch has stood for two centuries.

def: Paste of cement, sand and water bonding bricks or blocks.
ex: The bricklayer spread {{mortar}} evenly along each course.

def: Measuring positions, distances and levels of points on land.
ex: Accurate {{surveying}} set out the road centreline precisely.

def: Part of a road on which vehicles travel in one direction.
ex: The dual {{carriageway}} carries three lanes each way.

def: Circular junction where traffic moves one way around a central island.
ex: Traffic flows freely through the new {{roundabout}} at the junction.

def: Grade-separated junction letting roads cross without at-grade conflict.
ex: The motorway {{interchange}} keeps the two highways from crossing.

def: Long bridge of many spans carrying a road or railway across a valley.
ex: The railway {{viaduct}} strides across the valley on stone arches.

def: Wire cage filled with stones used for retaining or erosion control.
ex: A {{gabion}} wall protects the bank from river erosion.

def: Temporary watertight enclosure pumped dry for underwater construction.
ex: A {{cofferdam}} kept the pier foundation dry during the pour.

def: Outer covering fixed to a building for weather protection or appearance.
ex: Aluminium {{cladding}} gives the tower its sleek finish.

def: Strengthening or deepening an existing foundation to carry more load.
ex: The leaning wall needed {{underpinning}} to stop it sinking.

def: Welded grid of steel wires laid to reinforce a concrete slab.
ex: They laid {{reinforcement mesh}} across the floor before pouring.

def: Barrier in a wall stopping moisture rising through the masonry.
ex: A {{damp proof course}} keeps rising damp out of the walls.

def: Prestressing method where tendons are stressed after concrete has hardened.
ex: The bridge deck was strengthened using {{post-tensioning}} of steel tendons.

def: Prestressing method where tendons are tensioned before the concrete is cast.
ex: Precast beams commonly use {{pretensioning}} in a casting bed.

def: High-strength steel strand or bar used to prestress concrete members.
ex: Each {{tendon}} was grouted after stressing to prevent corrosion.

def: Strength value below which only a small percentage of results are expected.
ex: Concrete grade is defined by its {{characteristic strength}} at 28 days.

def: Ratio of water mass to cement mass in a concrete mix.
ex: A low {{water-cement ratio}} increases strength but reduces workability.

def: Period during which concrete is kept moist to develop strength.
ex: The specified {{curing time}} was seven days before formwork removal.

def: Steel manufactured with specific shapes for load-bearing structural use.
ex: The frame was fabricated from rolled {{structural steel}} sections.

def: Joint formed by fusing steel members together with molten filler metal.
ex: The beam-to-column {{weld connection}} was inspected by ultrasonic testing.

def: Joint where steel members are fastened together using bolts.
ex: A {{bolted connection}} allows easier field assembly than welding.

def: Zone in a member where full plastic yielding allows rotation under load.
ex: A {{plastic hinge}} forms at the beam ends during collapse analysis.

def: Design condition relating to collapse or failure of a structure.
ex: The column was checked against the {{ultimate limit state}} for buckling.

def: Design condition concerning deflection, cracking and comfort under normal use.
ex: Deflection was limited to satisfy the {{serviceability limit state}}.

def: Ratio of steel reinforcement area to the concrete cross-sectional area.
ex: The {{reinforcement ratio}} must exceed the code minimum value.

def: Closed loop of bar providing shear reinforcement in beams and columns.
ex: Each {{stirrup}} was spaced at 150 mm along the beam.

def: Classification of concrete based on its characteristic compressive strength.
ex: A {{grade of concrete}} C30/37 was specified for the columns.

def: Instrument used to measure depth of concrete over embedded reinforcement.
ex: A {{cover meter}} confirmed adequate cover to the rebar.

def: Voids in concrete caused by mortar not filling around aggregate.
ex: Poor compaction produced {{honeycombing}} on the column face.

def: Separation of coarse aggregate from mortar in fresh concrete.
ex: Excessive vibration caused {{segregation}} of the concrete mix.

def: Rise of water to the surface of freshly placed concrete.
ex: Excess water caused {{bleeding}} on the fresh slab surface.

def: Facility where concrete ingredients are weighed and mixed in batches.
ex: Ready-mix concrete was delivered from the nearby {{batch plant}}.

def: Tool that consolidates fresh concrete by removing entrapped air.
ex: A poker {{vibrator}} was used to compact the deep pour.

def: Duration for fresh concrete to change from plastic to rigid state.
ex: Retarders extend the {{set time}} in hot weather.

def: Admixture that slows the setting rate of concrete.
ex: A {{retarder}} was added to delay setting during long hauls.

def: Admixture that speeds up setting and early strength gain of concrete.
ex: An {{accelerator}} allowed early formwork stripping in cold weather.

def: Admixture that improves workability without increasing water content.
ex: A {{plasticizer}} improved flow while keeping strength high.

def: Deliberate introduction of tiny air bubbles to improve frost resistance.
ex: {{Air entrainment}} protects the pavement against freeze-thaw damage.

def: Siliceous material that reacts with lime to form cementitious compounds.
ex: Fly ash acts as a {{pozzolan}} in blended cements.

def: Fine residue from coal combustion used as a cement replacement.
ex: {{Fly ash}} reduced the heat of hydration in the mass pour.

def: Cement blended with ground granulated blast-furnace slag for durability.
ex: {{Slag cement}} improved durability in the marine environment.

def: Heat released as cement reacts chemically with water.
ex: High {{heat of hydration}} can crack thick concrete elements.

def: Weak plane formed when fresh concrete is placed on hardened concrete.
ex: A delay in pouring created a {{cold joint}} in the wall.

def: Planned interface between two successive placements of fresh concrete.
ex: A {{construction joint}} was located at mid-span of the slab.

def: Groove that controls where shrinkage cracking occurs in concrete.
ex: A {{contraction joint}} was sawn every five metres in the slab.

def: Bar length needed to transfer stress to concrete by bond.
ex: The {{development length}} ensured the bar would not pull out.

def: Overlap of two bars to transfer force through surrounding concrete.
ex: A {{lap splice}} joined the column bars at the floor level.

def: Resistance to slip between reinforcement and surrounding concrete.
ex: Ribbed bars provide higher {{bond strength}} than plain bars.

def: Measured opening of a crack, limited for durability and appearance.
ex: The design limited {{crack width}} to 0.3 millimetres.

def: Reduction in concrete volume as it dries and loses moisture.
ex: Drying {{shrinkage}} caused hairline cracks in the slab.

def: Gradual increase in strain under sustained long-term load.
ex: Concrete {{creep}} increases deflection of prestressed beams over time.

def: Ratio of stress to strain in the elastic range of a material.
ex: Steel has a higher {{modulus of elasticity}} than concrete.

def: Ratio of lateral strain to axial strain under load.
ex: Concrete typically has a {{Poisson's ratio}} of about 0.2.

def: Weakening of material under repeated cyclic loading over time.
ex: Steel bridge welds were checked for {{fatigue}} cracking.

def: Structural steel shape formed by hot rolling in a mill.
ex: A universal beam is a common {{rolled section}}.

def: Horizontal outer plate of an I-section resisting bending stress.
ex: The top {{flange}} carries compressive stress in the beam.

def: Vertical plate connecting the flanges and resisting shear.
ex: The beam {{web}} was stiffened to prevent buckling.

def: Plate welded to a member to prevent local buckling.
ex: A web {{stiffener}} was added at the support point.

def: Steel plate connecting several members at a truss joint.
ex: The diagonal members were bolted to a {{gusset plate}}.

def: Device joining steel and concrete to act compositely.
ex: Headed stud {{shear connector}} units bonded the deck to the girder.

def: System where steel and concrete act together structurally.
ex: {{Composite construction}} combined a steel beam with a concrete slab.

def: Triangular weld joining two surfaces at right angles.
ex: A {{fillet weld}} connected the plate to the flange.

def: Weld joining two aligned plates edge to edge.
ex: A full penetration {{butt weld}} joined the girder segments.

def: Effective thickness of a weld resisting the applied stress.
ex: The {{weld throat}} governed the strength of the fillet.

def: Tension induced in a bolt during tightening before service load.
ex: High-strength bolts require a specified {{preload}} for slip resistance.

def: Bolted joint that resists load through friction between surfaces.
ex: A {{slip-critical connection}} was used where movement was unacceptable.

def: Equivalent pin-ended length used in column buckling calculations.
ex: End restraints reduced the column's {{effective length}}.

def: Geometric property relating bending moment to extreme fibre stress.
ex: A larger {{section modulus}} reduces bending stress in the beam.

def: Property describing distribution of area about an axis for buckling.
ex: A small {{radius of gyration}} lowers the buckling load.

ru: плоская форма потери устойчивости при изгибе
def: Sideways buckling of an unrestrained beam under bending.
ex: Restraints were added to prevent {{lateral-torsional buckling}}.

def: Factored load used in strength checks of a structure.
ex: The {{design load}} was obtained by multiplying by partial factors.

def: Variable load from occupancy, furniture and movable equipment.
ex: The floor was designed for an {{imposed load}} of five kilonewtons.

def: Load on a roof from accumulated snow, based on region.
ex: The roof truss resisted the design {{snow load}}.

def: Factor applied to loads or material strengths in limit-state design.
ex: A {{partial safety factor}} accounts for material variability.

def: Load value with a defined probability of not being exceeded.
ex: The {{characteristic load}} was multiplied by a partial factor.

def: Section where concrete crushing and steel yielding occur simultaneously.
ex: A {{balanced section}} defines the boundary of failure modes.

def: Section where steel yields before concrete crushes, giving ductile failure.
ex: An {{under-reinforced section}} warns of failure through cracking.

def: Section where concrete crushes before steel yields, failing suddenly.
ex: An {{over-reinforced section}} fails in a brittle manner.

def: Permissible stress used in the allowable stress design method.
ex: The {{working stress}} method limits stress to a safe fraction.

def: Slab spanning and carrying load primarily in one direction.
ex: The corridor was built as a {{one-way slab}}.

def: Slab carrying load in two perpendicular directions to supports.
ex: The square panel behaved as a {{two-way slab}}.

def: Reinforced slab supported directly on columns without any beams.
ex: A {{flat slab}} allowed lower storey heights.

def: Slab with a grid of ribs on its underside to reduce weight.
ex: A {{waffle slab}} spanned the large column-free hall.

def: Thickened slab region around a column resisting punching shear.
ex: A {{drop panel}} increased shear capacity at each column.

def: Short cantilever projecting from a column to support a beam.
ex: The precast beam rested on a concrete {{corbel}}.

def: Removal of formwork once concrete has gained sufficient strength.
ex: {{Formwork stripping}} began after the concrete reached target strength.

def: Assembly of tied reinforcing bars placed before concreting.
ex: The {{rebar cage}} was lowered into the pile bore.

def: Compression test of cast concrete cubes to verify strength.
ex: The {{cube test}} confirmed the concrete met specification.

def: Device estimating concrete surface strength by measuring rebound.
ex: A {{rebound hammer}} gave a quick strength estimate on site.

def: Reaction of carbon dioxide with concrete, reducing its alkalinity.
ex: {{Carbonation}} lowered the cover's protection against corrosion.

def: Process of making raw water safe and suitable for drinking or use
ex: The city built a modern {{water treatment}} plant on the river.

def: Removal of contaminants from used water before discharge or reuse
ex: Effluent flows to the {{wastewater treatment}} facility for processing.

def: Settling of suspended solid particles out of water by gravity
ex: Heavy particles settle during {{sedimentation}} in the clarifier tank.

def: Adding chemicals so fine particles clump together for easier removal
ex: Alum is dosed to promote {{coagulation}} of colloidal solids.

def: Gentle mixing that gathers coagulated particles into larger settleable flocs
ex: Slow stirring during {{flocculation}} forms visible floc particles.

def: Passing water through a porous medium to remove suspended solids
ex: Sand {{filtration}} removes the remaining fine particles from the water.

def: Adding chlorine to water to kill pathogens and disinfect it
ex: Final {{chlorination}} ensures the drinking water is free of bacteria.

def: Destroying harmful microorganisms in water to make it safe
ex: UV {{disinfection}} inactivates viruses without adding chemicals.

def: Liquid waste or treated water discharged into a body of water
ex: Treated {{effluent}} must meet standards before entering the river.

def: Raw water or wastewater entering a treatment plant for processing
ex: The {{influent}} enters the plant through the inlet screen.

def: Oxygen microbes need to decompose organic matter in water
ex: High {{biochemical oxygen demand}} indicates heavy organic pollution.

def: Biological floc of microbes used to treat wastewater aerobically
ex: The {{activated sludge}} process breaks down organic pollutants biologically.

def: Semi-solid residue that settles out during water or wastewater treatment
ex: The {{sludge}} is pumped to a digester for further treatment.

def: Introducing air into water to supply oxygen for biological treatment
ex: Diffused {{aeration}} keeps the microbes in the tank alive.

def: Water that is safe and suitable for human drinking
ex: The treatment plant supplies {{potable water}} to the whole district.

def: Cloudiness of water caused by suspended particles reducing clarity
ex: High {{turbidity}} makes the water look murky and unfit to drink.

def: Science of the movement, distribution and quality of water on Earth
ex: The dam design relied on detailed {{hydrology}} of the basin.

def: Rainwater that flows over land instead of soaking into soil
ex: Heavy rain increases surface {{runoff}} into the storm drains.

def: Process by which water on the ground soaks into the soil
ex: Permeable pavement improves {{infiltration}} and reduces flooding.

def: Land area where all rainfall drains to a common outlet
ex: The reservoir collects water from a large {{catchment area}}.

def: Ridge of land separating waters flowing to different rivers or basins
ex: Rainfall on each side of the {{watershed}} drains to different rivers.

def: Surface water produced by rainfall or melting snow during storms
ex: The {{storm water}} is diverted into an underground detention tank.

def: Measures and structures used to prevent or reduce flooding damage
ex: Levees and channels form part of the {{flood control}} scheme.

def: Flat land beside a river that is subject to flooding
ex: Building is restricted on the river {{floodplain}} to avoid damage.

def: Raised embankment built along a river to prevent flooding
ex: The {{levee}} held back the rising river during the storm.

def: Low barrier across a river that controls flow and measures discharge
ex: Water spills evenly over the {{weir}} into the channel below.

def: Rate of change of hydraulic head over distance driving water flow
ex: A steep {{hydraulic gradient}} increases the seepage flow rate.

def: Height of a water column representing the energy driving flow
ex: The pump must overcome the total {{hydraulic head}} of the system.

def: Volume of water flowing past a point per unit time
ex: The river {{discharge}} rises sharply after heavy rainfall.

def: Constructed waterway that delivers water to agricultural fields
ex: Farmers open the gate to fill the {{irrigation channel}}.

def: Artificial supply of water to land for growing crops
ex: Drip {{irrigation}} delivers water directly to the plant roots.

def: Underground layer of rock or sediment that holds and yields water
ex: The town draws its supply from a deep {{aquifer}}.

def: Downward movement of water through soil and porous rock layers
ex: Slow {{percolation}} recharges the underlying aquifer over time.

def: Flow of water with a free surface exposed to atmosphere
ex: Manning's equation describes the {{open channel flow}} velocity.

def: Smooth fluid motion in parallel layers without disruption between them
ex: At low velocity the pipe exhibits steady {{laminar flow}}.

def: Chaotic fluid motion with irregular fluctuations and mixing eddies
ex: High velocity produces {{turbulent flow}} inside the penstock.

def: Dimensionless value predicting whether flow is laminar or turbulent
ex: A high {{Reynolds number}} indicates the flow is turbulent.

def: Reduction in hydraulic energy due to friction and fittings
ex: Friction causes significant {{head loss}} along the long pipeline.

def: Large pipe carrying water under pressure to a turbine
ex: Water drops through the {{penstock}} to spin the turbine.

def: Structure that dissipates energy of fast water below a spillway
ex: The {{stilling basin}} absorbs the energy of the falling water.

def: Sudden rise in water level where fast flow becomes slow
ex: A {{hydraulic jump}} forms at the toe of the spillway.

def: Low barrier across a river to raise and divert water
ex: The {{barrage}} raises the water level for the intake canal.

def: Structure that draws water from a source into a system
ex: The {{intake structure}} screens debris before water enters the pipe.

def: Point where a drain or sewer discharges into a water body
ex: Treated effluent leaves the plant through the coastal {{outfall}}.

def: Tank that removes sand and grit from incoming wastewater
ex: Sand settles out in the {{grit chamber}} at the plant inlet.

def: Tank where suspended solids settle out to clarify water
ex: Sludge collects at the bottom of the {{clarifier}}.

def: Bed of media over which wastewater trickles for biological treatment
ex: Microbes on the {{trickling filter}} media consume organic waste.

def: Sealed tank where microbes break down sludge producing biogas
ex: Sludge is stabilised in the anaerobic {{digester}}.

def: Occurring in the absence of free oxygen, as in decomposition
ex: The {{anaerobic}} process digests sludge without any oxygen.

def: Requiring or occurring in the presence of free oxygen
ex: The {{aerobic}} tank supplies oxygen for microbial growth.

def: Oxygen needed to chemically oxidise pollutants in a water sample
ex: The lab measures {{chemical oxygen demand}} to gauge water pollution.

def: Mass of particles suspended in water retained by filtration
ex: High {{total suspended solids}} reduces the clarity of the effluent.

def: Amount of oxygen gas dissolved in water available to aquatic life
ex: Low {{dissolved oxygen}} threatens fish in the polluted stream.

def: Excess nutrients causing algal overgrowth and oxygen depletion in water
ex: Fertiliser runoff triggers {{eutrophication}} in the shallow lake.

def: Network delivering safe drinking water to consumers
ex: The new pipeline expands the town's {{potable water supply}}.

def: System of pipes delivering treated water to users across an area
ex: Leaks in the {{distribution network}} waste treated water.

def: Principal pipe carrying water in a public supply system
ex: A burst {{water main}} flooded the street overnight.

def: Drain designed to carry away surface runoff from rainfall
ex: Leaves clogged the {{storm drain}} during the downpour.

def: Basin that temporarily stores stormwater to reduce peak flows
ex: The {{detention pond}} holds runoff and releases it slowly.

def: Site where river flow and water level are continuously measured
ex: The {{gauging station}} records the river's daily discharge.

def: Graph showing how river discharge varies with time
ex: The flood {{hydrograph}} peaks a few hours after the storm.

def: Average interval between floods of a given magnitude
ex: The bridge is designed for a hundred-year {{return period}} flood.

def: Combined water loss from soil evaporation and plant transpiration
ex: High {{evapotranspiration}} reduces the water available for runoff.

def: Water falling from clouds as rain, snow, sleet or hail
ex: Annual {{precipitation}} determines the reservoir's yield.

def: Forcing water through a membrane to remove dissolved salts
ex: Seawater is desalinated using {{reverse osmosis}} membranes.

def: Removing dissolved salts from seawater to produce fresh water
ex: The coastal plant supplies fresh water through {{desalination}}.

def: Removing large solids from wastewater using bars or mesh
ex: Coarse {{screening}} traps rags and debris at the inlet.

def: Device that intercepts fats and oils before they enter sewers
ex: The kitchen's {{grease trap}} prevents blockages downstream.

def: Underground tank treating household sewage by settling and digestion
ex: Rural homes rely on a {{septic tank}} for sewage disposal.

def: Contaminated liquid draining from a landfill or waste heap
ex: A liner prevents {{leachate}} from polluting the groundwater.

def: Chemical, physical and biological characteristics of water for a use
ex: Regular sampling monitors the river's {{water quality}}.

def: Pipe system carrying domestic wastewater to a treatment plant
ex: The house connects to the municipal {{sanitary sewer}}.

def: Minerals and salts dissolved in water measured by evaporation
ex: High total {{dissolved solids}} gives the water a salty taste.

def: Reversing flow to clean trapped solids from a filter bed
ex: The sand filter needs a {{backwash}} to remove clogging.

def: Facility housing pumps that lift water or wastewater through a system
ex: The {{pump station}} lifts sewage over the ridge to the plant.

def: Baseline sewage flow occurring without any rainfall contribution
ex: The plant is sized above the average {{dry weather flow}}.

def: Land bordering the edge of a river channel
ex: Erosion is undermining the soft {{riverbank}} near the bend.

def: Gradual accumulation of fine sediment reducing a reservoir's capacity
ex: {{Siltation}} has reduced the storage volume of the reservoir.

def: Movement of vehicles along a road over a given period of time.
ex: Engineers measured the {{traffic flow}} at the busy intersection during rush hour.

def: Total number of vehicles passing a point during a specified time interval.
ex: The survey recorded high {{traffic volume}} on the main highway.

def: Qualitative measure describing operating conditions and driver satisfaction on a road.
ex: The road operates at a poor {{level of service}} during peak periods.

def: Maximum number of vehicles a road can carry under prevailing conditions.
ex: Adding lanes increased the {{road capacity}} of the corridor.

def: Curve in the horizontal plane connecting two straight road sections.
ex: A gentle {{horizontal curve}} guided the road around the hillside.

def: Curve in the vertical plane joining two different road gradients.
ex: The {{vertical curve}} smoothed the transition between the two slopes.

def: Banking of a road on a curve to counteract centrifugal force.
ex: The {{superelevation}} on the bend helped vehicles turn safely at speed.

def: Length of road visible ahead to a driver at any moment.
ex: Trees were cleared to improve the {{sight distance}} on the curve.

def: Convex slope of a road surface allowing water to drain sideways.
ex: The road's {{camber}} directed rainwater into the roadside channels.

def: Pavement of bituminous layers that distributes load through granular layers.
ex: The engineers chose a {{flexible pavement}} for the new access road.

def: Pavement of concrete slabs that spreads load over a wide area.
ex: A {{rigid pavement}} was laid to withstand heavy truck traffic.

def: Top pavement layer directly in contact with vehicle tyres.
ex: A new {{wearing course}} was laid to restore the road surface.

def: Painted lines and symbols guiding and regulating road traffic.
ex: Fresh {{road marking}} clearly separated the two traffic lanes.

def: Planning the layout where two or more roads meet.
ex: Careful {{junction design}} reduced accidents at the crossroads.

def: Maximum flow of vehicles a roundabout can accommodate per hour.
ex: The study estimated the {{roundabout capacity}} under peak demand.

def: Structure of rails and supports on which trains run.
ex: Workers replaced a worn section of {{railway track}} overnight.

def: Transverse support holding rails at the correct gauge and level.
ex: Concrete {{sleeper}} units replaced the old timber ones.

def: Crushed stone bed supporting and draining a railway track.
ex: Fresh {{ballast}} was packed around the sleepers to hold them firm.

def: Point where a road crosses a railway at the same level.
ex: Barriers lowered at the {{level crossing}} as the train approached.

def: System controlling train movements to ensure safe separation.
ex: Modern {{signalling}} allowed trains to run more closely together.

def: Condition where excess vehicles cause slow or halted movement.
ex: Rush-hour {{traffic congestion}} added an hour to the journey.

def: Ratio comparing peak-hour flow to the busiest quarter-hour rate.
ex: A high {{peak hour factor}} showed steady flow across the hour.

def: Mean number of vehicles using a road each day annually.
ex: The {{average daily traffic}} justified widening the road to four lanes.

def: Number of vehicles occupying a unit length of road.
ex: High {{traffic density}} reduced the average speed of vehicles.

def: Speed of vehicles when traffic is light and unimpeded.
ex: The {{free-flow speed}} on the motorway approached the design limit.

def: Distance needed for a driver to see and stop safely.
ex: The design ensured adequate {{stopping sight distance}} before the junction.

def: Distance needed for a vehicle to overtake another safely.
ex: Limited {{passing sight distance}} banned overtaking on that stretch.

def: Curve of gradually changing radius between a straight and a bend.
ex: A {{transition curve}} eased vehicles smoothly into the sharp bend.

def: Vertical curve where the road rises then falls over a summit.
ex: Sight distance over the {{crest curve}} was carefully checked.

def: Vertical curve where the road dips then rises through a low point.
ex: Headlight range governed the design of the {{sag curve}} at night.

def: Speed used to determine the geometric features of a road.
ex: The {{design speed}} set the minimum radius for every curve.

def: Strip of road alongside the carriageway for stopping or emergencies.
ex: The driver pulled onto the {{shoulder}} to change a flat tyre.

def: Central strip separating opposing streams of traffic.
ex: A raised {{median}} prevented vehicles from crossing into oncoming lanes.

def: British term for the median separating carriageways on a road.
ex: Crash barriers ran along the {{central reservation}} of the motorway.

def: Marked strip of carriageway for a single line of vehicles.
ex: The right {{traffic lane}} was closed for resurfacing work.

def: Intermediate asphalt layer between the base and wearing course.
ex: The {{binder course}} was compacted before the surface layer was laid.

def: Uppermost pavement layer providing a smooth riding surface.
ex: A fine {{surface course}} gave the road a smooth finish.

def: Layer beneath the base course spreading load to the subgrade.
ex: A well-compacted {{sub-base}} supported the pavement above.

def: Mixture of bitumen and graded aggregate used for paving.
ex: Hot {{asphalt concrete}} was spread and rolled across the roadway.

def: Thin bitumen film bonding a new layer to an existing one.
ex: A {{tack coat}} was sprayed before laying the new asphalt.

def: Bitumen application sealing and bonding a granular base layer.
ex: The {{prime coat}} penetrated the base before paving began.

def: Bowl-shaped hole in a road surface caused by wear.
ex: The council filled the {{pothole}} that had damaged several tyres.

def: Permanent grooves formed in a pavement under wheel loads.
ex: Heavy trucks caused visible {{rutting}} in the asphalt surface.

def: Ability of a road surface to grip vehicle tyres.
ex: Good {{skid resistance}} reduced accidents on the wet bend.

def: Process of determining pavement layer thicknesses and materials.
ex: The {{pavement design}} accounted for the expected heavy axle loads.

def: Board displaying information or instructions to road users.
ex: A warning {{traffic sign}} alerted drivers to the sharp bend.

def: Set of coloured lights controlling vehicle movement at junctions.
ex: The {{traffic signal}} turned red and the cars stopped.

def: Designated place for people to walk safely across a road.
ex: Children waited at the {{pedestrian crossing}} for the light to change.

def: Pedestrian crossing marked with broad white stripes on the road.
ex: The pedestrian stepped onto the {{zebra crossing}} and traffic halted.

def: Part of a road reserved for the use of cyclists.
ex: A painted {{cycle lane}} kept riders separate from cars.

def: Traffic lane reserved mainly for buses and public transport.
ex: Cars are fined for driving in the {{bus lane}} during peak hours.

def: Arrangement letting roads cross at different vertical levels.
ex: A {{grade separation}} removed conflicts between the two busy roads.

def: Short road allowing vehicles to enter or leave a motorway.
ex: The car left the motorway using the next {{slip road}}.

def: Lane letting entering vehicles speed up before merging with traffic.
ex: Drivers use the {{acceleration lane}} to match the motorway speed.

def: Lane letting exiting vehicles slow down before leaving the road.
ex: The truck moved into the {{deceleration lane}} before the exit.

def: Road segment where merging and diverging traffic streams cross.
ex: Conflicts in the {{weaving section}} slowed the merging traffic.

def: Safety barrier restraining vehicles that leave the carriageway.
ex: The {{crash barrier}} stopped the car from sliding down the embankment.

def: Metal rail along a road edge to protect errant vehicles.
ex: The bus hit the {{guardrail}} but stayed on the road.

def: Physical measures that reduce vehicle speeds in residential areas.
ex: {{Traffic calming}} measures cut speeds through the village.

def: Raised section of road forcing drivers to slow down.
ex: A {{speed hump}} slowed cars near the school entrance.

def: Legal strip of land reserved for a road or railway.
ex: The road authority acquired the {{right of way}} before construction.

def: Smallest circle a vehicle can turn within on a road.
ex: The junction was widened to suit the {{turning radius}} of lorries.

def: Residential street closed at one end with a turning area.
ex: The children played safely in the quiet {{cul-de-sac}}.

def: All vehicles that move on a railway, powered or unpowered.
ex: The operator invested in new {{rolling stock}} for the line.

def: Distance between the inner faces of two railway rails.
ex: The line uses the standard {{track gauge}} of 1,435 millimetres.

def: Connection where two lengths of railway rail meet.
ex: A worn {{rail joint}} caused the familiar clatter of the wheels.

def: Track arrangement allowing a train to switch to another line.
ex: The train slowed over the {{turnout}} as it changed tracks.

def: Overhead wire system supplying electric power to trains.
ex: The train drew current from the {{catenary}} above the track.

def: Raised area beside a track where passengers board trains.
ex: Passengers waited on the {{railway platform}} for the delayed train.

def: Railway yard where freight wagons are sorted into trains.
ex: Wagons were shunted in the {{marshalling yard}} to form the freight train.

def: Finished railway track including rails, sleepers and ballast.
ex: The {{permanent way}} was inspected for defects each week.

def: Extra banking a curve would need for a train's actual speed.
ex: High {{cant deficiency}} on the bend limited the train's speed.

def: Bridge carrying one road or railway over another.
ex: The new {{flyover}} eased congestion at the busy junction.

def: Road or path passing beneath another road or railway.
ex: Pedestrians used the {{underpass}} to avoid the busy highway.

def: Area on a road where drivers pay a passage charge.
ex: Traffic queued at the {{toll plaza}} to pay the motorway fee.

def: Total weight carried by one axle of a vehicle.
ex: The bridge had a limit on the permitted {{axle load}}.

def: Textured road strip that vibrates a straying vehicle's wheels.
ex: A {{rumble strip}} warned drifting drivers to correct their course.

def: Reflective post marking the edge or alignment of a road.
ex: Reflective {{delineator}} posts traced the road's edge at night.

def: Scheduling technique identifying the longest sequence of dependent tasks determining project duration.
ex: The planner used the {{critical path method}} to find the shortest possible completion time.

def: Timetable listing activities, durations and dates required to complete a project.
ex: The {{project schedule}} showed the foundation work starting in early March.

def: Significant point or event marking progress within a project schedule.
ex: Completing the roof was a major {{milestone}} in the building programme.

def: Bar chart displaying project tasks against time to visualise the schedule.
ex: Each activity appeared as a horizontal bar on the {{Gantt chart}}.

def: Formal offer to carry out work at a stated price in competition.
ex: Five firms submitted a {{tender}} for the new hospital contract.

def: Process of obtaining goods, services and contractors needed for a project.
ex: The {{procurement}} team selected suppliers for all the structural steel.

def: Company or person hired to carry out construction work under contract.
ex: The main {{contractor}} was responsible for delivering the whole building.

def: Firm hired by the main contractor to perform a specialised part of work.
ex: An electrical {{subcontractor}} installed all the cabling on site.

def: Inspecting a finished building to list minor defects needing correction.
ex: During {{snagging}} the surveyor noted several unpainted doors.

def: Formal transfer of a completed building from contractor to the client.
ex: The {{handover}} took place once all systems were fully commissioned.

def: Time after completion when the contractor must repair emerging defects free.
ex: A leak appeared within the {{defects liability period}} and was fixed free.

def: Document setting out how site risks and worker safety are managed.
ex: The {{health and safety plan}} required helmets in every work zone.

def: Document describing step by step how a task is safely carried out.
ex: The crane lift required a detailed {{method statement}} before approval.

def: Systematic evaluation of hazards and measures to reduce potential harm.
ex: A {{risk assessment}} identified falling objects as the main hazard.

def: Approximate calculation of the total money required to complete a project.
ex: The initial {{cost estimate}} for the bridge was two million dollars.

def: Extra money or time reserved to cover unforeseen problems during a project.
ex: A ten percent {{contingency}} was added to cover unexpected ground conditions.

def: Formal instruction changing the scope, design or cost of contracted work.
ex: A {{variation order}} added two extra floors to the original design.

def: Instalment paid to a contractor for work completed at set stages.
ex: The client released a {{progress payment}} after the walls were finished.

def: Testing and adjusting building systems to ensure they work as designed.
ex: The {{commissioning}} of the ventilation system took two full weeks.

def: Revised drawing showing the structure exactly as actually constructed.
ex: The {{as-built drawing}} recorded where the pipes were finally installed.

def: Visit to a construction site to check work quality and safety.
ex: A weekly {{site inspection}} ensured the work met the specifications.

def: Detailed description of the tasks and deliverables a contract covers.
ex: The {{scope of work}} clearly excluded landscaping from the contract.

def: Hierarchical division of a project into smaller manageable work packages.
ex: The {{work breakdown structure}} split the tower into ten separate packages.

def: Time between ordering an item and its delivery to the site.
ex: The steel had a six-week {{lead time}} that delayed the frame.

def: Amount of time a task can slip without delaying the whole project.
ex: The non-critical activity had five days of {{float}} in the schedule.

def: Approved original plan used to measure later project performance against.
ex: Actual progress was compared with the {{baseline}} every month.

def: Tangible product or result that a project is required to produce.
ex: The signed drawings were the first {{deliverable}} of the design phase.

def: List of outstanding tasks and defects to fix before final acceptance.
ex: The {{punch list}} contained twenty small items to complete.

def: Managing the terms, payments and obligations of a construction contract.
ex: Good {{contract administration}} kept disputes on the project to a minimum.

def: Portion of payment withheld until defects are corrected after completion.
ex: The client held five percent {{retention}} until the snags were fixed.

def: Contract where the contractor completes all work for one fixed price.
ex: They chose a {{lump sum contract}} to fix the total cost early.

def: Procurement route where one firm both designs and constructs the project.
ex: The client used {{design and build}} to hold a single firm responsible.

def: Person or organisation who commissions and pays for the construction project.
ex: The {{client}} approved the design before construction began.

def: Professional providing expert design or technical advice on a project.
ex: A structural {{consultant}} reviewed the foundation calculations.

def: Any party with an interest in or affected by the project.
ex: Each {{stakeholder}} was consulted before the design was finalised.

def: Bringing labour, plant and equipment to site before work starts.
ex: {{Mobilisation}} included setting up cabins and delivering the excavators.

def: Drawing showing the layout of buildings and features on a site.
ex: The {{site plan}} indicated where each temporary office would stand.

def: Document stating required materials, standards and workmanship for the project.
ex: The concrete must meet the strength stated in the {{specification}}.

def: Checking materials and work to confirm they meet required standards.
ex: {{Quality control}} testing rejected a batch of weak concrete.

def: System of planned actions ensuring a project will meet quality requirements.
ex: The firm's {{quality assurance}} procedures prevented recurring defects.

def: Fault or flaw where work fails to meet the required standard.
ex: A cracked tile was recorded as a {{defect}} during inspection.

def: Short informal safety briefing given to workers before a task.
ex: The foreman held a {{toolbox talk}} on working at height.

def: Formal document authorising specific high-risk work under controlled conditions.
ex: A {{permit to work}} was required before entering the confined space.

def: Heavy machinery and equipment used to carry out construction work.
ex: The excavators and cranes are the main {{plant}} on this site.

def: General site costs like management, cabins and cranes not tied to items.
ex: Site security and welfare cabins were priced within the {{prelims}}.

def: Work paid by actual labour, plant and materials rather than fixed rates.
ex: Extra digging was charged as {{day works}} on an hourly basis.

def: Money allowed in a contract for work not yet fully defined.
ex: A {{provisional sum}} covered drainage that was still being designed.

def: Fixed sum the contractor pays for each day of late completion.
ex: The late handover triggered {{liquidated damages}} of a thousand daily.

def: Stage when the works are finished enough for the client to occupy.
ex: The certificate of {{practical completion}} was issued in June.

def: Measure comparing budgeted cost of work completed against actual progress.
ex: {{Earned value}} analysis showed the project running behind budget.

def: Movement of money into and out of a project over time.
ex: The {{cash flow}} forecast predicted a shortfall in the third month.

def: Adjusting the schedule to smooth demand for labour and equipment.
ex: {{Resource levelling}} avoided a sudden peak in crane demand.

def: Dividing a project into stages carried out one after another.
ex: The {{phasing}} allowed the first block to open before the rest.

def: Short-term plan detailing activities expected over the next few weeks.
ex: The three-week {{look-ahead schedule}} listed the upcoming concrete pours.

def: Daily record of weather, labour, events and progress on site.
ex: The rain delay was recorded in the {{site diary}} that afternoon.

def: Formal query from the contractor asking the designer to clarify details.
ex: A {{request for information}} was raised about the missing beam sizes.

def: Document or sample the contractor submits for the designer's approval.
ex: The tile {{submittal}} was approved before the order was placed.

def: Detailed drawing prepared by a fabricator showing how to make components.
ex: The steel {{shop drawing}} showed every bolt hole and connection.

def: Extent to which a design can be constructed easily and efficiently.
ex: Simplifying the joints improved the {{buildability}} of the frame.

def: Structures such as supports built temporarily to enable permanent works.
ex: The {{temporary works}} included falsework to hold the wet concrete.

def: Designated space on site for storing materials and equipment.
ex: Delivered steel was stacked neatly in the {{lay-down area}}.

def: Temporary fence enclosing a construction site for safety and security.
ex: Bright {{hoarding}} surrounded the site to keep the public out.

def: On-site provisions like toilets, canteens and rest areas for workers.
ex: The site provided clean {{welfare facilities}} including a heated canteen.

def: Person responsible for planning and delivering a project on time.
ex: The {{project manager}} coordinated all the trades on site daily.

def: Person overseeing daily construction operations and workers on site.
ex: The {{site manager}} checked that scaffolding was safe every morning.

def: Inspector appointed by the client to monitor workmanship and quality.
ex: The {{clerk of works}} rejected the poorly finished brickwork.

def: Detailed plan of construction activities set against a timeline.
ex: The {{programme}} showed the works finishing before the winter.

def: Approach maximising value and minimising waste in construction processes.
ex: {{Lean construction}} cut waste by improving material delivery timing.

def: Reviewing a design to achieve required functions at lower cost.
ex: {{Value engineering}} replaced the stone cladding with cheaper render.

def: Total agreed price payable to the contractor under the contract.
ex: The final {{contract sum}} rose after several variations were approved.

def: Gradual, uncontrolled growth of a project's work beyond the original scope.
ex: Constant extra requests caused serious {{scope creep}} and delays.

def: Set of manuals, certificates and drawings given to the client at completion.
ex: The {{handover documentation}} included warranties for all the equipment.

def: Introductory safety briefing every worker receives before starting on site.
ex: New workers completed a {{site induction}} before entering the compound.

def: Unplanned event that could have caused injury but did not.
ex: The falling brick was logged as a {{near miss}} on the report.

def: Complete set of drawings, specifications and terms forming the contract.
ex: Any dispute is settled by referring to the {{contract documents}}.

def: Defined group of related tasks awarded or managed as a single unit.
ex: The cladding {{work package}} was awarded to a specialist firm.

def: Project delivered fully complete and ready for the client to use.
ex: The factory was built as a {{turnkey project}} ready for production.

def: Regular document summarising work completed, costs and issues on a project.
ex: The monthly {{progress report}} confirmed the works were on schedule.

def: Regular safety check confirming that scaffolding remains stable and safe.
ex: A weekly {{scaffold inspection}} is required by safety regulations.

def: Screening bidders for competence and capacity before inviting their tenders.
ex: Only firms passing {{prequalification}} were invited to bid.

def: Statement settling the total sum finally due under the contract.
ex: The {{final account}} was agreed after all variations were valued.
